> [!WARNING] > ## Review limit reached > > You’ve reached a temporary PR review limit under our [Fair Usage Limits Policy](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/management/plans#fair-usage-limits-policy).
> Your recent review volume is higher than typical usage, so adaptive limits are currently applied. > > **Next review available in:** **2 minutes** > > Enable **[usage-based reviews](https://app.coderabbit.ai/settings/billing?tab=usage&orgId=17580d4c-7242-43bb-8e83-c902d1385296)** in Billing to review now. Otherwise, wait until the next included review is available. > You're only billed for reviews past your plan's rate limits ($0.25/file). > >
> How can I continue? > > After more reviews become available, a review can be triggered using the `@coderabbitai review` command as a PR comment. Alternatively, push new commits to this PR. > > To avoid repeated limits, reduce automatic review volume by pausing incremental auto-reviews earlier, using label-based review opt-in, excluding WIP or generated PR titles, or requesting reviews manually when the PR is ready. If your team needs uninterrupted high-volume reviews, an organization admin can enable usage-based reviews. > >
> > >
> How do review limits work? > > CodeRabbit enforces per-developer PR review limits for each organization. Most developers receive the normal plan review availability. > > For paid Pro and Pro+ PR reviews, CodeRabbit uses adaptive limits for sustained high-volume activity. When a developer's recent PR review activity reaches the 95th percentile or higher among CodeRabbit users, additional reviews become available more gradually as earlier reviews age out of the rolling window. > > Please refer [docs](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/management/plans#rate-limits) for additional details. > >
> >
> Review details > >
> ⚙️ Run configuration > > **Configuration used**: Organization UI > > **Review profile**: ASSERTIVE > > **Plan**: Pro > > **Run ID**: `a6caf035-b90d-43e4-a4e1-bb0ba2b96021` > >
> >
> 📥 Commits > > Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between d6ee22c5739506e3d2921d034a39a328cbdb6048 and f704fee5ee4d63673909d00c90c2b1a9082e669c. > >
> >
> ⛔ Files ignored due to path filters (1) > > * `uv.lock` is excluded by `!**/*.lock` > >
> >
> 📒 Files selected for processing (20) > > * `.github/workflows/ci.yml` > * `README.md` > * `design_tokens.json` > * `main.py` > * `pyproject.toml` > * `src/data/loader.py` > * `src/render/pipeline.py` > * `src/svg/cards/action.py` > * `src/svg/cards/money.py` > * `src/svg/cards/property_.py` > * `src/svg/cards/rent.py` > * `src/svg/cards/wildcard.py` > * `src/svg/components/badge.py` > * `src/svg/components/circle_title.py` > * `src/svg/components/m_glyph.py` > * `src/text/measure.py` > * `src/text/richtext.py` > * `tests/conftest.py` > * `tests/test_render.py` > * `tests/test_svg_structure.py` > >
> >

> [!WARNING]
> Gemini encountered an error creating the review. You can try again by commenting `/gemini review`.

**4 issues found** across 21 files
Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues) ```text Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately. P3: The badge center moved from x=135 to x=138, but `badge_clear_x` in the `property_header` call still uses the old value `135 + 62 + 12`. Update it to `138 + 62 + 12` (new center x + radius + gap) so the header text clear zone stays aligned with the badge's actual position. P3: The `# pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]` comments on both `value_badge` calls look unnecessary — `tokens.chrome()` is typed to return `str`, which is compatible with the `fill: str | None` parameter. The same inline pattern (`fill=tokens.chrome("property_body")`) is already used on line 202 without any suppression. Adding superfluous type-ignore comments reduces the value of type-checking by masking real issues. Consider removing the two suppress comments unless pyrefly is producing a confirmed false positive for this specific pattern (in which case a brief why-comment would help future readers). P2: The space between `ignore` and `[missing-import]` breaks Pyrefly's specific-error-code suppression syntax. With the space, Pyrefly reads this as a generic `# pyrefly: ignore` (suppresses **all** errors on the next line) and treats `[missing-import]` as inert trailing text — so the comment is misleading about what it actually suppresses. P1: The README says bundled font files are committed under `src/assets/fonts/`, but only licenses are present — the actual `.otf`/`.ttf` files are missing. This means `--fonts bundled` mode and any code path that calls `FontRole.measure_path` (used for text measuring before line breaking) will fail. Add the referenced font files (`GilliusADFNo2-Regular.otf`, `GilliusADFNo2-Bold.otf`, `Oswald-Bold.ttf`) to the fonts directory so the documentation matches reality and the bundled-fonts pipeline actually works. ```
You're on the cubic free plan with 10 free PR reviews remaining this month. [Upgrade for unlimited reviews](https://cubic.dev/settings?tab=subscription).

[Re-trigger cubic](https://www.cubic.dev/action/re-review/pr/hbmartin/deal-deck-generator/4/ai_pr_review_1783525578700_796e7e41-1ee0-4959-8cc0-91df0548b8b3?returnTo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fhbmartin%2Fdeal-deck-generator%2Fpull%2F4)

<!-- cubic:v=2620c7f2-80da-41fb-a399-68f260f5b1c6 -->
<!-- cubic:review-run=58bcf274-6c90-4932-917e-0b34ba3094ac -->
<!-- metadata:{"confidence":9} -->
P1: The README says bundled font files are committed under `src/assets/fonts/`, but only licenses are present — the actual `.otf`/`.ttf` files are missing. This means `--fonts bundled` mode and any code path that calls `FontRole.measure_path` (used for text measuring before line breaking) will fail. Add the referenced font files (`GilliusADFNo2-Regular.otf`, `GilliusADFNo2-Bold.otf`, `Oswald-Bold.ttf`) to the fonts directory so the documentation matches reality and the bundled-fonts pipeline actually works.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

```text
Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At README.md, line 60:

<comment>The README says bundled font files are committed under `src/assets/fonts/`, but only licenses are present — the actual `.otf`/`.ttf` files are missing. This means `--fonts bundled` mode and any code path that calls `FontRole.measure_path` (used for text measuring before line breaking) will fail. Add the referenced font files (`GilliusADFNo2-Regular.otf`, `GilliusADFNo2-Bold.otf`, `Oswald-Bold.ttf`) to the fonts directory so the documentation matches reality and the bundled-fonts pipeline actually works.</comment>

<file context>
@@ -1,531 +1,92 @@
-```bash
-uv run black . && uv run ruff check .
-```
+The SVGs use hybrid stacks: system **Gill Sans** is preferred when present
+(macOS), with bundled free fallbacks committed under `src/assets/fonts/`
+(Gillius ADF No2 for body text, Oswald for condensed headings; licenses
</file context>
```

</details>

<!-- cubic:v=edbc82b3-0c06-4894-b80f-97f2f2bea75c -->
<!-- cubic:review-run=58bcf274-6c90-4932-917e-0b34ba3094ac -->
<!-- metadata:{"confidence":9} -->
P2: The space between `ignore` and `[missing-import]` breaks Pyrefly's specific-error-code suppression syntax. With the space, Pyrefly reads this as a generic `# pyrefly: ignore` (suppresses **all** errors on the next line) and treats `[missing-import]` as inert trailing text — so the comment is misleading about what it actually suppresses.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

```text
Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At main.py, line 5:

<comment>The space between `ignore` and `[missing-import]` breaks Pyrefly's specific-error-code suppression syntax. With the space, Pyrefly reads this as a generic `# pyrefly: ignore` (suppresses **all** errors on the next line) and treats `[missing-import]` as inert trailing text — so the comment is misleading about what it actually suppresses.</comment>

<file context>
@@ -2,6 +2,7 @@
 
 import sys
 
+# pyrefly: ignore [missing-import]
 from src.cli import main
 
</file context>
```

</details>

```suggestion
# pyrefly: ignore[missing-import]
```

<!-- cubic:v=fa3a4be0-d878-4834-a363-c2b48e20b240 -->
<!-- cubic:review-run=58bcf274-6c90-4932-917e-0b34ba3094ac -->
<!-- metadata:{"confidence":8} -->
P3: The badge center moved from x=135 to x=138, but `badge_clear_x` in the `property_header` call still uses the old value `135 + 62 + 12`. Update it to `138 + 62 + 12` (new center x + radius + gap) so the header text clear zone stays aligned with the badge's actual position.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

```text
Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At src/svg/cards/property_.py, line 56:

<comment>The badge center moved from x=135 to x=138, but `badge_clear_x` in the `property_header` call still uses the old value `135 + 62 + 12`. Update it to `138 + 62 + 12` (new center x + radius + gap) so the header text clear zone stays aligned with the badge's actual position.</comment>

<file context>
@@ -44,8 +45,15 @@ def build_property(card: PropertyCard, deck) -> core.SVGDocument:
+        tokens.chrome("badge_ring_property"),
+        fill=tokens.chrome("property_body"),
+    )
+    badge.set("transform", core.translate(138, 124))
     doc.add(badge)
 
</file context>
```

</details>

<!-- cubic:v=168fda72-add1-4c4b-a653-b5e34915fde5 -->
<!-- cubic:review-run=58bcf274-6c90-4932-917e-0b34ba3094ac -->
<!-- metadata:{"confidence":7} -->
P3: The `# pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]` comments on both `value_badge` calls look unnecessary — `tokens.chrome()` is typed to return `str`, which is compatible with the `fill: str | None` parameter. The same inline pattern (`fill=tokens.chrome("property_body")`) is already used on line 202 without any suppression. Adding superfluous type-ignore comments reduces the value of type-checking by masking real issues. Consider removing the two suppress comments unless pyrefly is producing a confirmed false positive for this specific pattern (in which case a brief why-comment would help future readers).

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

```text
Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At src/svg/cards/wildcard.py, line 218:

<comment>The `# pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]` comments on both `value_badge` calls look unnecessary — `tokens.chrome()` is typed to return `str`, which is compatible with the `fill: str | None` parameter. The same inline pattern (`fill=tokens.chrome("property_body")`) is already used on line 202 without any suppression. Adding superfluous type-ignore comments reduces the value of type-checking by masking real issues. Consider removing the two suppress comments unless pyrefly is producing a confirmed false positive for this specific pattern (in which case a brief why-comment would help future readers).</comment>

<file context>
@@ -214,10 +214,13 @@ def _build_two_color(card: WildcardCard, deck) -> core.SVGDocument:
     ring = tokens.chrome("badge_ring_property")
-    badge = value_badge(doc, tokens, card.value, ring_color=ring)
+    body = tokens.chrome("property_body")
+    # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]
+    badge = value_badge(doc, tokens, card.value, ring_color=ring, fill=body)
     badge.set("transform", core.translate(*BADGE_POS))
</file context>
```

</details>
